title: American Chopsuey

subs.name: 150 g rice noodles for a gluten-free version

ingredients.name: eggs
visual_checks: 1 per serving, fried sunny-side-up on top
ingredients.notes: 1 per serving, fried sunny-side-up on top

subs.name: skip the egg for a vegetarian version — mark as vegetarian
subs.notes: the dish is still complete without the egg

ingredients.name: spring onion, whites and greens separated

subs.name: 2 tbsp tamarind pulp mixed with 1 tbsp sugar for a more tangy version

ingredients.name: oil
visual_checks: for deep frying noodles plus 2 tbsp for gravy and 1 tsp per egg
ingredients.notes: for deep frying noodles plus 2 tbsp for gravy and 1 tsp per egg

steps.text: Boil the noodles in salted water for 3 minutes — they should be barely cooked, with significant bite. Drain thoroughly, spread on a clean towel and pat dry. The drier the noodles, the crispier they will fry.

Heat oil in a deep kadai to 180 °C. Divide the dried noodles into 3 portions and slide one portion at a time into the oil, pressing lightly with a ladle to form a flat disc. Fry 3–4 minutes until deeply golden and crackling. Drain on a rack — the noodle disc will harden further as it cools.
Mix cornflour with 3 tbsp cold water into a smooth slurry. Combine ketchup, soy sauce, vinegar, chilli sauce, sugar and black pepper in a small bowl.

Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok or kadai on high flame. Add garlic and spring onion whites and stir for 20 seconds. Add onion and stir-fry for 1 minute, then add carrot, cabbage and capsicum and cook for 3 minutes on high heat until just tender.

Pour the sauce blend and 250 ml hot water into the wok and bring to a boil. Stir in the cornflour slurry and cook 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the gravy is thick, glossy and tangy — it should coat the back of a spoon. Adjust salt.
Fry the eggs sunny-side-up in a little oil in a separate small pan — the yolks should be set on the edges but still runny in the centre. Season lightly with salt and pepper.

Place a crispy noodle disc on each plate. Ladle the hot vegetable gravy generously over so the noodles begin to soften at the edges while the centre stays crunchy. Perch a fried egg on top, scatter spring onion greens and serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → skip the egg for a vegetarian version — mark as vegetarian (the dish is still complete without the egg); noodles → 150 g rice noodles for a gluten-free version; tomato-ketchup → 2 tbsp tamarind pulp mixed with 1 tbsp sugar for a more tangy version; soy-sauce → coconut aminos
